definition: Represent each subject or condition by a functional or structural connectivity matrix, flatten the comparable edge weights, and compute correlation or cosine similarity between edge vectors. For SPECTRA, this audits whether brain-model performance depends on evaluation connectomes being close to training connectomes.
example_usage: python vector_correlation_similarity.py --train train_connectomes.csv --eval eval_connectomes.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --feature-cols-prefix edge_

citations.title: Learning and comparing functional connectomes across subjects

evidence.summary: The review frames functional connectomes as brain interaction graphs and discusses strategies for comparing them across subjects.
The paper shows subject-level correlation matrices and inter-subject variability as a basis for comparing connectomes.

limitations: Connectivity estimates are sensitive to parcellation, denoising, motion, scanner, and global signal choices.
Flattened edge correlation ignores graph topology unless topology-aware metrics are added.
Subject similarity can be confounded by acquisition site or demographic variables.
quality_gates: Report parcellation, preprocessing, edge definition, and whether negative edges were retained.
Run scanner/site and motion curves alongside connectome-similarity curves when available.
Check whether matrix size or global connectivity strength alone explains the similarity axis.
